summary: Early Netherlandish/Flemish painter, student of Robert Campin (Master of Flemalle). Best known for The Descent from the Cross (commissioned by the Crossbowmen's/Archers' Guild in Leuven), the Braque Triptych, the Beaune Altarpiece (Last Judgement), and St. Luke Drawing the Virgin.

works.name: The Descent from the Cross (c. 1435)
works.description: Panel painting in the Museo del Prado, commissioned by the Crossbowmen's (Archers') Guild of Leuven. The most frequently clued work.

clues.clue: Commissioned by the Crossbowmen's/Archers' Guild of Leuven; crossbow imagery (spandrels, body shape) references this

commissioned by the Confraternity of the Archers of Leuven for their chapel

clues.clue: Blue-clad Mary faints/swoons, mirroring Christ's pose

clues.clue: A skull (of Adam) on the ground near the scene

Saint Nicodemus gazes at a skull at the bottom of this painting
a man with a black headdress...stares across the title action at the skull of Adam

clues.clue: A servant climbs a ladder whose foot is in front of the cross but whose top is behind it
examples: depicted a servant that climbs a ladder whose foot is in front of the cross, but whose top is behind it

clues.clue: Man in red catches swooning woman; a man in a black headdress with cloth-of-gold robe appears
examples: a man in red catches a swooning woman in a bright blue robe

clues.clue: Implied shape of the composition is a crossbow — an implied 'T' shape enclosed by a gold box
examples: tightly enclosed by a gold box
an implied shape of a crossbow in this painting

works.name: Braque Triptych
works.description: Devotional triptych commissioned by a family from Tournai. Shows Jesus holding a globus cruciger in the center, with John the Baptist and Mary Magdalene on side panels.

clues.clue: When closed, shows a skull resting on a brick on the left and a cross on the right
examples: one of this man's altarpieces shows a skull and a cross when closed
can be closed to show a freaky skull and black cross
When closed, the Braque Triptych shows a cross on the right side, and one of these objects resting on a brick on the left

clues.clue: Center panel: Jesus holds a globus cruciger with bright orange halo; Mary and John the Evangelist flank him
examples: Christ stands in front of a bright orange (*) halo while holding a 'globus cruciger'
Its center panel depicts Jesus holding a globus cruciger with John the Evangelist on his left and the Virgin Mary on his right

clues.clue: Five floating Latin inscriptions across upper portion

clues.clue: Memorial for Jehan of Tournai (Braque family)
examples: Van der Weyden created this triptych as a memorial for Jehan of Tournai

works.name: Beaune Altarpiece / Last Judgement (c. 1445-50)
works.description: Massive nine-panel altarpiece (nonaptych) commissioned by Chancellor Nicolas Rolin for the Hospices de Beaune, depicting the Last Judgement.

clues.clue: Commissioned by Nicolas Rolin for the Hospices de Beaune
examples: commissioned by chancellor Nicolas Rolin to create an altarpiece depicting the Last Judgement for the Hospices de Beaune

works.name: Miraflores Altarpiece
works.description: Triptych containing panels of the birth, death (central Pieta), and resurrection of Jesus. Named for its donation to a Spanish church.

works.name: Seven Sacraments Altarpiece
works.description: Triptych in Antwerp with a large crucifixion above the Eucharist in the center, using dramatic linear perspective in a cathedral interior.

works.name: Crucifixion Diptych

comprehensive_summary:
Rogier van der Weyden was an early Netherlandish painter and student of Robert Campin (the Master of Flemalle), active at the Burgundian court of Philip the Good. He was a contemporary of Jan van Eyck and the teacher of Hans Memling. His most frequently clued work is The Descent from the Cross, a panel painting now in the Museo del Prado, commissioned by the Crossbowmen's (Archers') Guild of Leuven. The painting features Christ's body splayed in the shape of a crossbow, a blue-clad Virgin Mary swooning in a pose that mirrors Christ's, Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus lowering the body, the skull of Adam on the ground, and crossbow imagery in the spandrels. Erwin Panofsky called it a 'painted critique' of a work by van der Weyden's teacher.

The Braque Triptych, a memorial for Jehan of Tournai, shows Jesus holding a globus cruciger with a bright orange halo in the center, flanked by John the Baptist and Mary Magdalene, with five floating Latin inscriptions and a continuous landscape. When closed, it shows a skull on a brick and a cross. The Beaune Altarpiece (Last Judgement), a massive nine-panel work commissioned by Chancellor Nicolas Rolin for the Hospices de Beaune, shows Jesus on a rainbow above the Archangel Michael weighing souls, and was likely painted in response to the Ghent Altarpiece.

St. Luke Drawing the Virgin depicts a saint in red robes using silverpoint to draw Mary breastfeeding, with a triple-arched view onto a garden, loggia, and river that resembles van Eyck's Madonna of Chancellor Rolin. The Miraflores Altarpiece contains scenes of Christ's birth, death (with a central Pieta where Mary holds Jesus diagonally), and resurrection. The Seven Sacraments Altarpiece features angels in progressively darker clothing accompanying each sacrament, with a central crucifixion in dramatic linear perspective within a cathedral interior. The Crucifixion Diptych unites two panels with a continuous gray wall and recurring red cloth. Other notable works include portraits of Charles the Bold (with Order of the Golden Fleece), Francesco d'Este, Lady Wearing a Gauze Headdress, The Magdalen Reading, and the Thyssen Madonna.
